exercises.title: Map annotations to schema elements
exercises.instructions:
Match each Spring for GraphQL annotation/element to what it maps to in the GraphQL schema:

A: @QueryMapping method named 'product'
B: @MutationMapping method named 'createProduct'
C: @SchemaMapping(typeName='Product', field='reviews') method
D: @Argument String id on a method parameter
E: src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls
exercises.expected_solution:
A: @QueryMapping 'product'
   Maps to: type Query { product(...): ... } in the schema
   Method name matches schema field name exactly
   Method is called when client queries: { product(id: "P1") { ... } }

B: @MutationMapping 'createProduct'
   Maps to: type Mutation { createProduct(input: ...): ... }
   Method name matches mutation field name
   Called when client sends: mutation { createProduct(input: {...}) { ... } }

C: @SchemaMapping(typeName='Product', field='reviews')
   Maps to: type Product { reviews: [Review!]! } — a field on the Product type
   Called once per Product in the result when client requests { product { reviews { } } }
   WARNING: called N times for N products (N+1 risk)

D: @Argument String id
   Maps to: the 'id' argument in schema: product(id: ID!): Product
   Spring auto-extracts 'id' from the GraphQL query arguments
   For input types: @Argument CreateProductInput input → auto-deserializes the input map

E: src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls
   The GraphQL Schema Definition Language file
   Spring for GraphQL reads all *.graphqls files from resources/graphql/ at startup
   Schema defines all types; Java methods implement the resolver logic

test_cases.expected:
A: @QueryMapping 'product'
   Maps to: type Query { product(...): ... } in the schema
   Method name matches schema field name exactly
   Method is called when client queries: { product(id: "P1") { ... } }

B: @MutationMapping 'createProduct'
   Maps to: type Mutation { createProduct(input: ...): ... }
   Method name matches mutation field name
   Called when client sends: mutation { createProduct(input: {...}) { ... } }

C: @SchemaMapping(typeName='Product', field='reviews')
   Maps to: type Product { reviews: [Review!]! } — a field on the Product type
   Called once per Product in the result when client requests { product { reviews { } } }
   WARNING: called N times for N products (N+1 risk)

D: @Argument String id
   Maps to: the 'id' argument in schema: product(id: ID!): Product
   Spring auto-extracts 'id' from the GraphQL query arguments
   For input types: @Argument CreateProductInput input → auto-deserializes the input map

E: src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls
   The GraphQL Schema Definition Language file
   Spring for GraphQL reads all *.graphqls files from resources/graphql/ at startup
   Schema defines all types; Java methods implement the resolver logic

hints: @QueryMapping: top-level Query fields only
@SchemaMapping: field resolvers on non-Query types
schema.graphqls: loaded from classpath:graphql/ directory

exercises.title: Complete the Spring GraphQL controller
exercises.instructions: Fill in the Spring for GraphQL controller annotations.

blanks.hint: extracts GraphQL argument into Java param
field resolver receives the parent object

hints: @Controller: not @RestController for GraphQL (different HTTP handling)
@Argument: extracts both scalar and input type arguments
Field resolver parameter: the parent object (Product for Product.reviews)

exercises.title: Minimal Spring GraphQL setup
exercises.instructions:
Write minimal Spring for GraphQL setup:
1. schema.graphqls content: type Query { hello: String! }
2. HelloController: @QueryMapping hello() returns 'Hello from GraphQL!'
3. @SpringBootTest: verify controller exists
4. application.yml: enable GraphiQL
5. Document: where schema file goes
hints: Schema file: src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls
GraphiQL: spring.graphql.graphiql.enabled=true
@QueryMapping method name = schema Query field name

exercises.title: @QueryMapping with arguments
exercises.instructions:
Write ProductController with argument handling:
1. product(id): @Argument String id
2. products(category): @Argument String category (nullable)
3. searchProducts(keyword, limit): two @Argument params
4. Show: how @Argument maps GraphQL ID! to Java String
5. @SpringBootTest: controller methods exist
hints: ID! schema type → String Java type (GraphQL ID serialized as String)
Nullable arg: @Argument String category — null if client didn't provide it
Integer (boxed) for nullable Int argument; int (primitive) for non-null Int!

exercises.title: @MutationMapping with input types
exercises.instructions:
Write ProductMutationController:
1. createProduct(@Argument CreateProductInput input) → returns Product
2. updatePrice(@Argument String productId, @Argument Double newPrice) → Product
3. deleteProduct(@Argument String id) → Boolean
4. CreateProductInput record: name, priceAmount, category, sellerId
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): create, update price, delete returns true
hints: CreateProductInput record: field names match GraphQL input type fields
@Argument CreateProductInput input: Spring auto-deserializes GraphQL input map to record
Boolean (boxed): for nullable Boolean! use Boolean (boxed) or boolean (primitive for non-null)

exercises.title: @SchemaMapping for field resolvers
exercises.instructions:
Write field resolvers for Product type:
1. @SchemaMapping Product.reviews: fetches reviews for a product
2. @SchemaMapping Product.seller: fetches seller info
3. Show N+1 risk: each product triggers a separate DB call
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): reviews resolver, seller resolver
hints: @SchemaMapping(typeName='Product'): field resolver on Product type (method name = field name)
N+1: each product triggers one call — 10 products = 10 DB calls
Solution: @BatchMapping in T3 — batch all productIds into one query

exercises.title: GraphQL error handling
exercises.instructions:
Write GraphQL error handling:
1. ProductNotFoundException extends RuntimeException
2. @ControllerAdvice GraphQlExceptionHandler: handle ProductNotFoundException
3. Return GraphQlError with INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR or NOT_FOUND extension
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): exception thrown, error mapped correctly
hints: DataFetcherExceptionResolverAdapter: Spring's base class for GraphQL exception mapping
ErrorType.NOT_FOUND: maps to 404 conceptually (GraphQL has no HTTP status)
Return null: let default handler manage other exceptions

exercises.title: @GraphQlTest with GraphQlTester
exercises.instructions:
Write @GraphQlTest for the ProductController:
1. Use @GraphQlTest to load only GraphQL layer
2. @MockBean the ProductService
3. GraphQlTester.document() to execute a query
4. .path() to assert on response fields
5. @GraphQlTest (2 tests): product found, product not found returns null
hints: @GraphQlTest: slice test — faster than @SpringBootTest, loads only GraphQL layer
graphQlTester.document(): specify the GraphQL query string
.path('product.name').entity(String.class).isEqualTo(): assert nested field value

exercises.title: Pagination in GraphQL resolvers
exercises.instructions:
Implement cursor-based pagination for Flipkart products:
1. ProductPage record: items, total, page, size, hasNext
2. @QueryMapping products(@Argument ProductFilter filter, @Argument int page, @Argument int size)
3. Service: paginated product list from simulated DB
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): page 0 returns first batch, hasNext=true
hints: page * size: calculate offset
hasNext: (from + size) < total
ProductFilter2 as @Argument: Spring deserializes the GraphQL filter input to the record

exercises.title: Trace a GraphQL request through Spring
exercises.instructions:
Trace this GraphQL request through the Spring for GraphQL stack:

```
POST /graphql
{ 'query': 'query GetProduct($id: ID!) { product(id: $id) { name reviews { rating } } }',
  'variables': { 'id': 'PROD-001' } }
```

Explain exactly what Spring does, step by step, from HTTP request to JSON response.
exercises.expected_solution:
STEP 1: HTTP layer (DispatcherServlet)
  POST /graphql received
  Spring MVC routes to GraphQlHttpHandler (registered by starter)
  Request body parsed: { query: '...', variables: { id: 'PROD-001' } }

STEP 2: GraphQL Engine — parse + validate
  GraphQL parser: parses the query string to AST (Abstract Syntax Tree)
  Schema validation: checks 'product', 'name', 'reviews', 'rating' exist in schema
  Variable coercion: '$id' = 'PROD-001' (String → ID coercion)
  If invalid: returns { errors: [...] } immediately (no resolver called)

STEP 3: Execution — resolve fields
  Engine starts at Query type
  Calls: @QueryMapping product(@Argument String id='PROD-001')
    → ProductController.product('PROD-001')
    → productService.findById('PROD-001')
    → returns Product(id='PROD-001', name='iPhone 15', ...)

STEP 4: Resolve 'name' field
  Product has 'name: String!' in schema
  No @SchemaMapping for 'name' → Spring uses reflection to call getName()
    OR extracts field from Product record automatically
  → 'iPhone 15'

STEP 5: Resolve 'reviews' field
  Product has @SchemaMapping reviews(Product product) resolver
  Spring calls: reviewsController.reviews(product)
    → reviewService.findByProductId('PROD-001')
    → [Review(rating=5), Review(rating=4), Review(rating=3)]
  'rating' field: Spring calls getRating() on each Review
  Note: if query had 10 products, reviews() called 10 times (N+1!)

STEP 6: Build response
  GraphQL serializes the resolved tree to JSON:
  { 'data': { 'product': {
      'name': 'iPhone 15',
      'reviews': [{'rating': 5}, {'rating': 4}, {'rating': 3}]
  }}}

STEP 7: HTTP response
  Status: 200 (even if data is null/partial)
  Content-Type: application/json
  Body: the JSON above
  Note: GraphQL always returns 200 (errors in body, not HTTP status)

test_cases.expected:
STEP 1: HTTP layer (DispatcherServlet)
  POST /graphql received
  Spring MVC routes to GraphQlHttpHandler (registered by starter)
  Request body parsed: { query: '...', variables: { id: 'PROD-001' } }

STEP 2: GraphQL Engine — parse + validate
  GraphQL parser: parses the query string to AST (Abstract Syntax Tree)
  Schema validation: checks 'product', 'name', 'reviews', 'rating' exist in schema
  Variable coercion: '$id' = 'PROD-001' (String → ID coercion)
  If invalid: returns { errors: [...] } immediately (no resolver called)

STEP 3: Execution — resolve fields
  Engine starts at Query type
  Calls: @QueryMapping product(@Argument String id='PROD-001')
    → ProductController.product('PROD-001')
    → productService.findById('PROD-001')
    → returns Product(id='PROD-001', name='iPhone 15', ...)

STEP 4: Resolve 'name' field
  Product has 'name: String!' in schema
  No @SchemaMapping for 'name' → Spring uses reflection to call getName()
    OR extracts field from Product record automatically
  → 'iPhone 15'

STEP 5: Resolve 'reviews' field
  Product has @SchemaMapping reviews(Product product) resolver
  Spring calls: reviewsController.reviews(product)
    → reviewService.findByProductId('PROD-001')
    → [Review(rating=5), Review(rating=4), Review(rating=3)]
  'rating' field: Spring calls getRating() on each Review
  Note: if query had 10 products, reviews() called 10 times (N+1!)

STEP 6: Build response
  GraphQL serializes the resolved tree to JSON:
  { 'data': { 'product': {
      'name': 'iPhone 15',
      'reviews': [{'rating': 5}, {'rating': 4}, {'rating': 3}]
  }}}

STEP 7: HTTP response
  Status: 200 (even if data is null/partial)
  Content-Type: application/json
  Body: the JSON above
  Note: GraphQL always returns 200 (errors in body, not HTTP status)

hints: GraphQL always returns 200 HTTP (errors in response body)
Validation BEFORE execution: invalid field → error, no resolver called
N+1 visible at step 5: reviews() called per product

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay payment GraphQL controller
exercises.instructions:
Implement Razorpay payment GraphQL resolvers:
1. @QueryMapping payment(id), payments(merchantId, status)
2. @MutationMapping createPayment(input), initiateRefund(input)
3. @SchemaMapping Payment.merchant field resolver
4. @GraphQlTest or @SpringBootTest (3 tests): queries, mutation, field resolver
hints: @SchemaMapping typeName='Payment': field resolver on Payment type
merchant field resolver: receives PaymentRecord parent, returns MerchantRecord
ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe for shared state in tests

exercises.title: Context variables and authenticated user
exercises.instructions:
Access authenticated user in GraphQL resolvers:
1. Get current user from SecurityContext or GraphQL context
2. @QueryMapping myOrders(): returns orders for currently authenticated user
3. Don't accept userId as argument (security: use from auth context)
4. @SpringBootTest: authenticated user extracted from context
hints: GraphQlContext: Spring's thread-local context for GraphQL request
Never trust userId from @Argument — user can spoof it
@AuthenticationPrincipal: Spring Security's way to inject authenticated user

exercises.title: Complete Flipkart GraphQL API
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Flipkart product GraphQL API:
1. @QueryMapping: product, products (paginated), searchProducts
2. @MutationMapping: createProduct, updatePrice
3. @SchemaMapping: Product.reviews, Product.category
4. Error handling: ProductNotFoundException → GraphQL NOT_FOUND error
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): all operations work
hints: @SchemaMapping typeName='Product': field resolvers for Product.reviews and Product.category
paginate: skip(page*size).limit(size) from the products map
search: toLowerCase().contains(keyword) — case-insensitive search

exercises.title: GraphQL with JPA repository
exercises.instructions:
Connect Spring for GraphQL to JPA:
1. Product @Entity + ProductRepository (JpaRepository)
2. @QueryMapping product(id) → productRepository.findById(id)
3. @MutationMapping createProduct → productRepository.save()
4. Show: how Java entity fields map to GraphQL schema
5. @DataJpaTest: repository layer works
hints: @Entity field names: same as GraphQL schema field names → no extra mapping needed
findById returns Optional<Product>: GraphQL handles Optional transparently
No @SchemaMapping for simple fields: Spring extracts via reflection (get* methods)

exercises.title: Complete M27 T2 Spring GraphQL synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M27 T2 synthesis:
1. @QueryMapping + @MutationMapping + @SchemaMapping controller
2. Input type deserialization (@Argument)
3. Pagination (ProductPage)
4. Error handling (exception for not-found)
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): query, mutation, field resolver, pagination
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M27 T2 Spring for GraphQL synthesis
hints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe for shared state across tests
SynthPage: skip(page*size).limit(size) from map values
@SchemaMapping typeName='Item': must match schema type name exactly
